description: US-12 Manage the Waiting List — Strict first-come-first-served waiting list with a valid certificate required to join. When a slot frees up the longest-waiting applicant is named and offered for registration.

userStories.title: Waiting-list ordering rule
userStories.description: As a developer, I need first-come-first-served as a pure function, so the ordering is a property rather than a query detail. Source: tasks/prd-us-12-waiting-list.md §US-12.1
acceptanceCriteria: src/domain/customer/waitingList.ts exports nextInLine(entries, today) returning the entry with the earliest addedOn
Same-day ties break deterministically by insertion id, documented and never random
nextInLine returns the earliest entry regardless of certificate validity, and reports certificateExpired so the caller can flag it — the rule is never silently skipped
An empty list returns a typed WaitingListEmpty result, not undefined
Tests cover: single entry, ordering across dates, same-day tie, expired-certificate entry at the head
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Waiting-list use cases
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want to add, list, remove and promote waiting applicants, so applicants are treated in a fair, defensible order. Source: tasks/prd-us-12-waiting-list.md §US-12.2
acceptanceCriteria: addToWaitingList(deps, input) records name, date of birth, address, a free-text contact note, certificate type and validity, and addedOn from deps.clock.now()
It rejects an applicant whose certificate is already expired with a typed CertificateExpired error
listWaiting(deps) returns entries in strict arrival order, each flagged with whether its certificate has since expired
removeFromWaitingList(deps, {entryId, reason}) requires a reason and writes an audit entry
promoteFromWaitingList(deps, {entryId}) returns a registration draft plus the certificate-expired flag; it does not itself register anyone
Promotion rejects with NoFreeCustomerNumber when no slot is free
Test proves a failing registration leaves the entry in place — the entry is removed only after registration succeeds
Tested against hand-written fakes and a fake clock
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: WaitingListEntry schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need waiting-list entries persisted with removals retained, so the order of past promotions stays reconstructable. Source: tasks/prd-us-12-waiting-list.md §US-12.3
acceptanceCriteria: Prisma model WaitingListEntry: id, firstName, lastName, birthDate, address fields, contactNote String?, certificateType, certificateValidUntil, addedOn, removedOn DateTime?, removalReason String?
Removed entries are retained with a removedOn timestamp rather than deleted
Index on addedOn; the active-list query filters removedOn IS NULL
Entries do not reference or occupy a Customer row
Integration test asserts arrival ordering survives a same-day tie
Migration committed under prisma/migrations/
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Waiting-list screen at /warteliste
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want to manage the waiting list and be told when a slot frees up. Source: tasks/prd-us-12-waiting-list.md §US-12.4
acceptanceCriteria: Route /warteliste lists entries in arrival order with position, name, date added, days waiting, and a German badge when the certificate expired while waiting
The list has no sort or reorder controls — the order is the rule
The add form validates with Zod and refuses an already-expired certificate with a specific German message
When at least one customer number is free, a prominent banner names the longest-waiting applicant and offers 'Jetzt registrieren', opening the pre-filled registration form
An expired-certificate applicant at the head shows a warning before the registration form opens
'Entfernen' requires a reason and confirms before removing
All user-facing strings come from src/i18n/de.ts
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: E2E: full quota, waitlist, promotion
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Playwright spec proving the quota-to-waitlist-to-promotion chain. Source: tasks/prd-us-12-waiting-list.md §US-12.5
acceptanceCriteria: Spec sets the quota to 2 via the settings screen, fills both slots, attempts a third registration and asserts the user is directed to the waiting list
Spec adds two applicants, archives one customer, and asserts the banner names the first applicant added
Spec promotes them and asserts they receive the freed number and are removed from the list
Spec asserts the second applicant is now at the head
npm run test:e2e passes
Typecheck passes
